questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: How many positive integers $n$ are there such that $3 \leq n \leq 100$ and $x^{2^n}+x+1$ is divisible by $x^2+x$ + 1 ?
questions.solution:
Since $\omega$ and $\omega^2$ are factors of $\mathrm{X}^2+\mathrm{X}+1$; so $\omega$ and $\omega^2$ will be factors of $\mathrm{X}^{2 \mathrm{n}}+\mathrm{X}+1 \Rightarrow 2^n$ must be of the form $3 k+2 ; k \in I$. For this to happen, $n$ must be odd.
So $\mathrm{n}=\{3,5,7, \ldots \ldots 99\}$
Number of such numbers $=49$
